text: Hot work in areas with flammable gas requires continuous atmospheric monitoring. Gas readings within 80% of LEL threshold mandate work stoppage.
Manufacturer's obligation to disclose hazardous process information before permit issuance. Workers must be informed of all chemical hazards present in their work area.
Confined space entry requires a dedicated safety attendant, continuous gas monitoring, and a pre-entry checklist. Rescue plan must be in place before entry.
Shift changeover procedures must include a formal handoff of all active permits, ongoing maintenance activities, and current gas readings. No gap in supervision shall exceed 15 minutes.
The occupier shall ensure that every worker is provided with adequate safety training and personal protective equipment appropriate to the hazards in the workplace.
Emergency response plans must be tested every six months. Evacuation routes shall be clearly marked and kept unobstructed at all times. Assembly points must be designated for each zone.
In every factory, all plants, machinery and equipment shall be maintained in good repair and working condition. Any defect discovered shall be remedied without delay.
Gas detection systems shall be calibrated at intervals not exceeding three months. Sensor drift beyond 10% of span requires immediate recalibration or replacement.

Permit to work systems shall require written authorization for hot work, confined space entry, and high-risk maintenance. Permits shall be displayed at the work site and visible to all workers in the zone.
Where any worker is exposed to any substances which may be injurious to health, the occupier shall provide suitable respiratory protective equipment and ensure its proper use.

In all mines where flammable or toxic gases are likely to be encountered, continuous gas monitoring systems shall be installed at all active working faces, return air courses, and gas drainage pipelines. Alarm set points shall not exceed 25% of the lower explosive limit for flammable gases.

A comprehensive risk assessment shall be completed before the commencement of hot work in underground or surface mining operations. The assessment must consider methane layering, ventilation adequacy, presence of combustible dust, and the availability of fire suppression systems.

All shift changeovers in mines must include a mandatory gas reading review and equipment status handover. No miner shall be permitted to work in an area where gas readings have changed by more than 20% since the previous shift without a fresh risk assessment.

No person shall be permitted to enter a confined space in a mine until the atmosphere has been tested for oxygen content, flammable gases, and toxic contaminants. A confined space entry permit valid for one shift only shall be issued by the Mine Manager or authorised representative.

The workplace concentration of noxious gases shall be maintained below the threshold limit value. In coal mines, CO concentration shall not exceed 50 ppm in the general body of air. Continuous monitoring and recording of CO levels shall be carried out in all mechanised sections.

Emergency response plans for mines shall include provisions for fire, explosion, inundation, and toxic gas release. All underground workers shall carry self-contained self-rescuers (SCSR) with a minimum rated duration of 30 minutes. Evacuation drills shall be conducted at intervals not exceeding 90 days.

Mine operators shall implement a permit-to-work system for all high-risk activities including hot work, confined space entry, excavation near gas pipelines, and electrical maintenance in hazardous areas. Permits shall be reviewed and countersigned by the Mine Manager or delegated safety officer.
